response: Japan, a nation of approximately 125 million people, boasts a substantial economy with a GDP of $4.2 trillion, though growth remains modest at 1.1%, while unemployment stands at a remarkably low 2.6% and inflation is at 3.2%. The country carries a significant debt-to-GDP ratio of 263%, one of the highest in the world, yet maintains a strong export sector valued at $756 billion annually. Japan also ranks among the world's leaders in quality of life, reflected in its impressive life expectancy of 84.6 years.
